source_strategy: derive a development-only privacy-decision fixture from separate non-holdout survey and persona-reliability papers

population_definition.target_population: U.S. survey respondents represented through World Values Survey microdata and demographic conditioning stress tests
population_definition.sample_design: development-only benchmark synthesized from non-holdout survey-reliability studies
population_definition.notes: Based on a non-holdout WVS-backed reliability paper with more than 70K respondent-item instances, plus a Bangladesh low-resource comparison study.

reported_findings_template: persona prompting does not yield a clear aggregate improvement
some items and underrepresented subgroups experience disproportionate distortions
persona-conditioned outputs can redistribute error across demographic groups
low-resource persona generation can underperform humans sharply on culturally specific questions

human_reference_summary.source_paper: Assessing the Reliability of Persona-Conditioned LLMs as Synthetic Survey Respondents
human_reference_summary.secondary_source_paper: Misalignment of LLM-Generated Personas with Human Perceptions in Low-Resource Settings
human_reference_summary.reported_sample_or_scale: more than 70K respondent-item instances; Bangladesh questionnaire of 100 items with 2080 annotated persona responses
key_results: no clear aggregate improvement from persona prompting
distortions are heterogeneous across items and subgroups
human benchmark around 87% accuracy in the Bangladesh study
best LLM in the Bangladesh study reported around 61.7% accuracy
